title: Ensure AWS Security Hub is enabled

input_row.title: Ensure AWS Security Hub is enabled
input_row.description: Security Hub collects security data from various AWS accounts, services, and supported third-party partner products, helping you analyze your security trends and identify the highest-priority security issues. When you enable Security Hub, it begins to consume, aggregate, organize, and prioritize findings from the AWS services that you have enabled, such as Amazon GuardDuty, Amazon Inspector, and Amazon Macie. You can also enable integrations with AWS partner security products.
input_row.rationale: AWS Security Hub provides you with a comprehensive view of your security state in AWS and helps you check your environment against security industry standards and best practices, enabling you to quickly assess the security posture across your AWS accounts.
input_row.audit: Follow this process to evaluate AWS Security Hub configuration per region: From Console: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the AWS Security Hub console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/securityhub/. 2. On the top right of the console, select the target Region. 3. If the Security Hub > Summary page is displayed, then Security Hub is set up for the selected region. 4. If presented with "Setup Security Hub" or "Get Started With Security Hub," refer to the remediation steps. 5. Repeat steps 2 to 4 for each region. From Command Line: Run the following command to verify the Security Hub status: aws securityhub describe-hub This will list the Security Hub status by region. Check for a 'SubscribedAt' value. Example output: { "HubArn": "<security-hub-arn>", "SubscribedAt": "2022-08-19T17:06:42.398Z", "AutoEnableControls": true } An error will be returned if Security Hub is not enabled. Example error: An error occurred (InvalidAccessException) when calling the DescribeHub operation: Account <Account ID> is not subscribed to AWS Security Hub
input_row.remediation: To grant the permissions required to enable Security Hub, attach the Security Hub managed policy AWSSecurityHubFullAccess to an IAM user, group, or role. Enabling Security Hub: From Console: 1. Use the credentials of the IAM identity to sign in to the Security Hub console. 2. When you open the Security Hub console for the first time, choose Go to Security Hub. 3. The Security standards section on the welcome page lists supported security standards. Check the box for a standard to enable it. 4. Choose Enable Security Hub. From Command Line: 1. Run the enable-security-hub command, including --enable-default- standards to enable the default standards: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --enable-default-standards 2. To enable Security Hub without the default standards, include --no-enable-default-standards: aws securityhub enable-security-hub --no-enable-default-standards
input_row.impact: It is recommended that AWS Security Hub be enabled in all regions. AWS Security Hub requires that AWS Config be enabled.
input_row.default_value: By default, AWS Security Hub is not enabled. It must be manually set up in each region (and requires AWS Config) before findings can be aggregated or standards applied.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control regarding the enabling of AWS Security Hub is verifiable through AWS APIs and CLI commands. The `audit` field provided clear steps for checking the status of Security Hub via the AWS CLI command (`aws securityhub describe-hub`), which confirms its presence and status in the specified region. As the service configuration can be programmatically queried without the need for user intervention or visual inspection, the control is suitable for an automated check.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_securityhub_hub_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the AWS CLI command `aws securityhub describe-hub` to check the status of Security Hub.
- The AWS CLI should be run for each region of interest to ensure that Security Hub is enabled across the environment.
- The boto3 SDK method `securityhub.describe_hub()` can be employed in a Python script for programmatic evaluation.
- The target regions should be enumerated, and for each region, the Security Hub status will be evaluated.
- In the described output, check for the `SubscribedAt` field:
  - If `SubscribedAt` is present, Security Hub is enabled.
  - If the command returns an error related to disabled status (like `InvalidAccessException`), then it indicates Security Hub is not enabled.
- A clear Pass/Fail criterion:
  - Pass: `SubscribedAt` has a timestamp or an active status returned from the command.
  - Fail: any command error indicating that Security Hub is not enabled, or absence of `SubscribedAt`.

This automated approach ensures consistent checking of the Security Hub status across multiple AWS accounts and regions without manual intervention.
